description:
Estimate daily water demand across San Francisco zones using population and commercial square footage to identify conservation targets. Using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/water_demand.ipynb, analyze the 'households', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Relate households and buildings to zones (households link to buildings via building_id; buildings link to parcels via parcel_id; parcels contain zone_id). Calculate zone-level demand using SFPUC planning coefficients (55.0 gallons per person per day and 0.15 gallons per non-residential square foot per day) and compute gross per capita demand, treating missing values as 0.

Required outputs:
- Complete zone-level results: /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_water_demand.csv
- Top 15 zones by total demand: /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top15_total_demand.csv
- Top 15 zones by per capita demand (minimum 100 persons): /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top15_per_capita_demand.csv
- Summary JSON: /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/water_summary.json (containing citywide_total_demand_gpd, citywide_residential_demand_gpd, citywide_commercial_demand_gpd, zone_with_highest_demand, and zone_with_highest_per_capita)
- Scatter plot (total_persons vs. total_demand_gpd): /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/water_demand_scatter.png

The notebook must be fully executed.
